title: Predictions
content:
- Applications
  - 1/ Model aggregators vs. single-model apps
    - Model aggregators will take on R&D companies who only serve their own models.
    - The depth and quality of the user interface will determine the winners and losers.
    - Mainstream creators will prefer all-in-one apps.
  - 2/ Story-level tools
    - More innovation will happen to support the creative process end-to-end, including brainstorming, storyboard, clip generation, editing, and sound production.
  - 3/ Template ecosystems
    - The blank canvas problem will be solved: best AI artists will start creating templates that other AI creators can use.
    - Fully AI-generated video stocks will become widely available and compete against traditional stocks.
  - 4/ Real-time trends
    - GenAI apps will get connected to real-time trends (music, sounds, memes, characters).
    - This will allow users to create content that’s most relevant for the moment.
- Adoption
  - 5/ 10x growth in daily AI video creators
    - Millions more people will start making AI art for self-entertainment and self-expression.
    - Consumer-side adoption will far outpace B2B adoption in 2025.
  - 6/ Superstar AI creators
    - The “Mr Beast” or “Charlie D’Amelio” moment of AI creativity will happen in 2025.
    - Several AI creators will get to 1M+ followers.
    - This success will inspire millions more to start their AI creator journey.
  - 7/ New genres
    - We will see the explosion of crossovers (”Harry Potter + Star Wars”), adaptations, translations, and remakes of centuries of human culture.
    - New genres will emerge, including shortform series (1-5min episodes), sketch comedy, mini-podcasts, and AI music videos.
  - 8/ AI content factories
    - There will be continuous growth of small companies running systems of social media accounts with high volumes of content created with the help of AI.
  - 9/ “Me content”
    - There will be more and better ways to create content based on a user selfie or self-description.
    - Everyday people will be able to see full-length stories with themselves as protagonists.
  - 10/ Adult content creation
    - Some apps will embrace an adult-friendly approach and allow users to create NSFW content.
    - At least one adult-friendly GenAI app will develop an effective marketing strategy without explicit demos on traditional social media.
- Technology
  - 11/ Open source will close the gap on video
    - Open models like [Hunyuan], [CogVideoX], and [Mochi] will get even closer in quality to commercial models ([Sora](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/OpenAI#Sora), [Luma](https://venturebeat.com/ai/luma-ai-debuts-dream-machine-for-realistic-video-generation-heating-up-ai-media-race/), [Kling](https://venturebeat.com/ai/what-you-need-to-know-about-kling-the-ai-video-generator-rival-to-sora-thats-wowing-creators/), [Minimax](https://finance.yahoo.com/news/chinese-ai-tiger-minimax-launches-093000322.html)).
    - At least one open model will catalyze an ecosystem of fine-tunes and plugins.
  - 12/ Inference clouds
    - Specialist cloud providers will run models faster and cheaper.
    - Generation costs will fall 10x, $0.5 -> $0.05 per clip.
    - Custom training costs will also fall by 5-10x.
    - Lower costs will open AI video creation to a wider audience.
  - 13/ New inference architectures
    - More complex architectures will arise, combining multiple diffusion models, rule-based systems, test-time compute, character controls, community-trained nodes, and more.
  - 14/ Specialized models
    - Research focus will shift from foundational models to specialized tools: upscaling, regional editing, special effects, lip sync, and storyboarding.
    - A new category, "creative co-pilots," will emerge to assist creators through their entire creative process.
- Challenges
  - 15/ Copyright battles
    - People will continue creating using copyrighted characters.
    - Some IP holders will fight it, others will try to monetize it.
    - A new licensing standard will emerge; it will NOT be blockchain-based despite efforts to make it so.
  - 16/ On-device inference will not be impactful (yet)
    - It will take 2-3 years before substantial parts of the creative process move from the cloud to user devices.
  - 17/ No Hollywood breakthrough
    - GenAI will only play a supporting role in TV and film production, helping with individual scenes, special effects, and pre-production.
    - AI-native indie studios will take longer to succeed, with first mainstream releases in 2026 and beyond.
  - 18/ No killer AI game
    - There will be more pilots and demos for AI-generated games and worlds.
    - No AI-native game will be consistently played for its quality rather than the novelty factor.
  - 19/ No AI influencer
    - The original idea of an AI influencer will not live up to its hype.
    - Photorealistic human characters will not achieve celebrity-level influence and brand deal economics.
    - The most successful AI accounts will not be human simulators.
